awakener-0001.notes: Realm-flexible exalt-spam DPS/support whose kit changes per realm (poison in Aequor, counter in Ultra, embryo/keyflare in Caro/Chaos). Chaos 24 is a viable endgame single-target boss DPS; likes teammates that grant Aliemus to fuel her exalt spam.

contentNotes.arc1: Ultra 24 is very strong in Faded Legacy
contentNotes.arc2: Ultra 24 was nerfed hard; run her as Chaos/boss DPS

awakener-0002.notes: Alert-stalling poison tank. Exalt permanently stacks Alert (boosts teamwide shields over time) — the ideal support for Castor stall teams. Also generates embryo and triggers poison; at high investment Colorless Spiral makes her a poison DPS.

keyPairings.reason: Permanent Alert stacking from her Exalt scales Castor's shields, enabling indefinite stall

contentNotes.dtide: Strong stall enabler for long single-target waves

awakener-0003.notes: Best vulnerable applier in the game; her petrify effectively grants an extra turn each fight. Excellent keyflare and embryo generator; OE adds +25% Final DMG. Pairs especially well with glass-cannon DPS that need breathing room. 'Adding Aigis and mixing in Caro' is the easiest strong team for new players.

keyPairings.reason: Petrify buys a free setup turn for glass-cannon carries; vulnerable multiplies their burst

awakener-0004.notes: Anti-'bullshit' utility support — dispels Seals and discards enemy-inflicted symptom/status cards, while providing shields and vulnerable. At E3 a Precision-Slash/Combat-Stance burst DPS that wants a team making shields and copying/replaying her cards.

awakener-0005.notes: Multihit tentacle driver — triggers 25+ tentacle attacks in one Raging Waves turn, converting stacked STR and Tentacle DMG into burst. Her own damage is low, so she needs a team stacking STR/Tentacle DMG or a tentacle-hit payoff like Faros's Deep Currents.

keyPairings.reason: Deep Currents gives her 25+ tentacle hits a damage payoff

awakener-0006.notes: Offensive healer and counter driver — strong healing-over-time and consistent Vulnerable from his Exalt, and he can multiply Counter triggers for a turn. One of the rare endgame-viable Counter stackers (scales with STR and crits) given enough team buffs.

awakener-0007.notes: Deck-cycling card-trick support — fills your hand, replays cards from Ultra Space and sets up multi-card combo turns; staple of Aftershock teams. Her Astral Reign image relic makes her Exalt inflict permanent STR-down for stall strategies.

awakener-0008.notes: Corrosive shield-stall tank — hoards Onyx Plumes to accumulate end-of-turn shields; alone a great defensive support, with shield-buffers (Agrippa/Erica) he stalls the hardest bosses forever. Tosses Plumes for Corrosion, but needs another damage source to trigger it.

keyPairings.reason: Permanent Alert scales his Onyx-Plume shields for indefinite stall
Temp Alert per shield amplifies his stacking

awakener-0008.leapNote: His wheel triggers on Annihilation rather than on the Ultra Turn — relevant when building his Ultra Space play.

awakener-0009.notes: Extra-life and arithmetica support — her Exalt revives the team from death once per fight (every 6 turns at E3), letting a glass-cannon DPS be carried through mob fights to the boss. Also makes a little arithmetica and Tentacle DMG; retainable defensive skills keep the deck clean.

keyPairings.reason: Buys turns for ramping carries with her revive

awakener-0010.notes: The best keyflare generator and STR-stacking support in the game; her Exalt's unique debuffs are multiplicative with Weakness, Vulnerable and Final DMG. Excellent healer on Rouse, and at E2 her Exalt can end mob fights by itself. Best with STR-reliant DPS but improves almost any team.

awakener-0011.notes: Discard-abusing story-mode champion and core of discard teams — repeatedly discards to cut Lightning Retribution's cost, generating Pilots that rapidly stack STR and Tentacle DMG and shield the burst turn. Weakness+Vulnerable at E3; her 2-cost feels bad before E2/E3.

contentNotes.arc2: Soulforge makes her deal high damage to Aequor-tagged enemies (incl. chapter bosses)

awakener-0012.notes: Counter & poison combo witch — Skull Reaver scales with last hits early; at endgame her Thousand Mirages make tons of temp Counter/Poison for high-damage combo turns with Arachne/Nautila. OE puts 3 Mirages in hand for big burst.

keyPairings.reason: Thousand Mirages set up huge counter/poison combo turns
Converts her counter into AOE

awakener-0013.notes: The best Weakness applier among standard-banner characters and a cheap keyflare bot (high base Keyflare Regen + Life Drain). Equivalent Exchange discards enemy symptom/status cards; also light healing and Aliemus. Great for surviving harder content on a new account.

awakener-0014.notes: Burst DPS that snowballs with kills and excels at executing low-HP enemies; also stacks STR, applies vulnerable, and heals. Strong early but falls off against full-HP bosses with no minions — wants a partner that sets up kills.

keyPairings.reason: Feeds STR so Doresain's executes hit hard

contentNotes.dtide: Best on add-heavy waves; weak on single full-HP boss waves

awakener-0015.notes: Alert-stalling keyflare bot — free keyflare support whose Exalt inflicts Weakness and Vulnerable; on Rouse she makes temp Alert each time she shields, and Function Overload gives a huge shield + one-turn shielding buff. Core of Castor stall teams.

keyPairings.reason: Each Onyx Plume benefits from her temp Alert, letting Castor stack giant shields

awakener-0016.notes: Top-tier endgame Death-Resistance tank — her Exalt stacks DR to survive multiple lethal hits; the harder the fight, the better she is versus other defensive options. Fits any team that needs to live longer. (Tier-list support rank is B, but her endgame value is higher.)

keyPairings.reason: Standard boss-fight survivability pairing
Death-Resistance stacks fuel Mouchette's E1 stacking buff

awakener-0017.notes: Lemurian defensive card-draw support and AOE poison applier — her Exalt is a powerful card-selection tool plus a shield (set up a perfect opening hand). As a DPS she spams Deep Currents for AOE poison, best in full poison teams or with heavy tentacle-attack triggers.

awakener-0018.notes: Genesis Doll — boss-destroying Aliemus battery with big DMG-Amp buffs and Weakness+Vulnerable at E1. Deletes 25-30% of a boss's HP bar and boosts the team in Finale Form; shines in poison teams and solo-carries boss fights at OE.

keyPairings.reason: Standard boss-fight pairing for survivability
Core of poison teams

awakener-0019.notes: Genesis Helot — devastating bleed/Strike combo DPS. Stacking Crimson Shackles yields Bloodthirsty Flail (prepare+retain); copy it via The Lone Seed + Thais for one-shot Exalt turns. Needs E3 and The Lone Seed for consistency.

keyPairings.reason: Copies Bloodthirsty Flail to set up one-shot combo turns
Buys time while she sets up

contentNotes.dtide: High burst on boss waves with setup time

awakener-0020.notes: Genesis Ramona — time-looping toolbox. Exalt returns the last 3 cards you played for perfect setups; at E3 she can swap your posse to anything (e.g. Voices In Your Head for on-demand weak/vuln). Complex; her dual-posse DPS build is slow and not newbie-friendly.

awakener-0021.notes: Lemurian STR support and AOE mob clearer — with lots of Tentacle DMG his Usurp Exalt makes tons of temporary STR, the best offensive support for Astral Reign Lemurian teams with Tulu. STR steal has niche uses; his STR-scaling exalt cleans up low-HP enemies even at endgame.

awakener-0022.notes: Arithmetica printer and card copyist — draws cards, copies card effects, and banks +2 Arithmetica per turn on Rouse. Enables near-infinite combo turns; wants teammates that play many cards.

contentNotes.arc2: Falls off due to the per-turn card soft cap

awakener-0023.notes: Strike-DMG multiplier support: Exalt draws two Strikes and multiplies all Strike damage for the turn — fantastic for Strike DPS (Mouchette, GHelot). Can DPS via Shattering Strikes but needs heavy setup.

keyPairings.reason: Exalt draws Strikes and multiplies all Strike damage — fuels Mouchette's Strike combos
Same Strike-multiplier payoff

awakener-0024.notes: Universal multiplier aura support — a single card play gives a teamwide buff for the whole exploration (scales with her Death Resistance). Carol Psalm is strong Aliemus and lets you retain Arithmetica across turns; at E1 one of the most consistent Weakness/Vulnerable appliers in the game.

keyPairings.reason: Carol Psalm retains arithmetica across turns to set up her combo turns

awakener-0025.notes: Multihit crit-buff support — her Exalt and Rouse generate buckets of Crit DMG and Crit Rate for any crit DPS, and her AOE hits pop barriers / tank Chains of Resentment. Can main-DPS but needs high levels or STR buffs versus endgame bosses.

awakener-0026.notes: Arithmetica delivery service — Exalt banks 3 Arithmetica to spend any time (random buffs at E3) for characters that need lots of mana; applies decent poison in Astral Reign. OE makes her a top-tier Arithmetica support.

awakener-0027.notes: Tanky ramping juggernaut — generates big shields off her defense card and ramps Base DMG/STR via Fiamma cards and 0-cost Hyperflare resets until Hyperflare one-shots bosses. Needs E3 and her posse; wants STR/crit buffs and Arithmetica to fuel Combust cycles.

awakener-0028.notes: Dedicated healer that heals most when the team is low — ideal for low-HP-threshold carries (Uvhash, Sorel). Makes lots of embryo on Rouse and STR on OE; unlike Faint she has no long-exploration diminishing returns.

keyPairings.reason: Heals hard at low HP to keep sub-25%-HP carries alive
Sustains a squishy Exalt carry through back-to-back fights

awakener-0029.notes: Immortal poison tank — at E3 with Death Resistance stats she out-heals all incoming damage and can carry the team; also a surprising secondary DPS. The main shielder/healer for poison teams, but fits any team needing survivability.

awakener-0030.notes: High-maintenance poison DPS — once set up, Deadly Flames applies massive poison and she becomes the best sustained damage dealer on the standard banner. Finicky early; wants defense, Aliemus and STR from teammates, her posse, E1+, and a hand-size wheel on a support.

keyPairings.reason: Triggers her stacked poison

awakener-0031.notes: Strike-spamming Exalt DPS using 0-cost Blade of Defiance combos to power her Exalt. Good at mobs, struggles on high-HP bosses; OE deals +15% enemy Max HP, making her worth bringing purely for OE.

contentNotes.arc2: Works best in Astral Reign with Soulforge investment

awakener-0032.notes: The premier do-everything Aequor support — makes Aliemus, applies premium Weakness/Vulnerable debuffs, boosts team damage, heals at low HP and triggers poison. One of only two units (with Vortice) who can stack Realm Mastery in combat. Reliant on her Rouse; staple of GMurphy Lemurian teams but fits any team 'with blue in it'.

awakener-0033.notes: Death-defying glass-cannon carry — one of the highest-damage units in the game; melts story with Exalt + Mortal Blast combos. Needs E3 for full damage and loves a Death-Resistance team (her E1 stacks buffs each time DR triggers).

keyPairings.reason: Feeds and multiplies Strikes for her combos
Vulnerable + petrify set up her burst turn

awakener-0034.notes: Damage-delaying keyflare tank — her Exalt converts incoming damage into damage-over-time, letting an Aequor team survive massive hits, then self-cleanses with Vortex Reversal. Strong defensive keyflare bot with Weakness/Vulnerable and arithmetica.

keyPairings.reason: Not a Lemurian, but clears GMurphy's self-inflicted Sacrifice stacks

awakener-0035.notes: Genesis Murphy — stance-switching keyflare support-carry with powered-up tentacle stances at the cost of cooldowns and self-inflicted damage. Viable endgame DPS at OE/high investment. Her Soulforge scales with the number of OTHER Lemurians (Faros/Goliath/Miryam/Tulu): 1/2/3 → +6%/15%/30% Max HP and Shield.

keyPairings.reason: Base Murphy clears her self-inflicted Sacrifice stacks
Lemurian teammate for Soulforge scaling

awakener-0035.divineRealmNote: Triggers Divine Realm: Aequor — Tentacle DMG becomes 5% of team Max HP, Tentacle Gathering is removed, +50% team DMG Amp at exploration start (doubled for pure Aequor/Chaos), and Tranquil Sea / Raging Waves gain 3-turn cooldowns.

contentNotes.arc2: Lemurian Soulforge scaling only works in Astral Reign — her signature team is Arc-2 only

awakener-0036.notes: Shield-stall and counter support — her Exalt multiplies the team's existing shield (great for Sanga/Alva), converts Counter into AOE damage, and at E3 blocks incoming debuffs.

keyPairings.reason: Exalt multiplies existing shields — huge for shield-scaling teammates
Same shield-multiply payoff

awakener-0037.notes: Poison-trigger keyflare support — Exalt instantly triggers poison (speeding up poison teams) and boosts teamwide poison in Astral Reign; high base Keyflare Regen makes her a solid keyflare bot. Best in full poison teams.

awakener-0038.notes: Defensive STR buffer — Exalt gives a burst of temporary STR (good for STR-reliant DPS) plus shields. Scales hard with investment and is excellent at +12 / level 90, but outclassed before that.

awakener-0039.notes: Counter-removal bot — strips enemy Counter with Wax-Hot Desire and can be an early-game Counter DPS (converts counter to damage in Faded Legacy). Caught in the Astral Reign Ultra-24 nerf, so unusable as a DPS for most endgame now.

awakener-0040.notes: Buff-stacking sub-DPS — Discovers relics and spams Chromaphage for AOE damage and a big one-turn temp-STR boost to the main DPS. Wants an Exalt-reliant carry that doesn't need arithmetica. Voracious Resolve posse highly recommended.

keyPairings.reason: Builds STR and massively buffs her Exalt with Chromaphage temp STR

awakener-0041.notes: Scaling glass-cannon combo DPS — Atonement by Pain boosts his first card each turn and grows after every fight, making him incredible in long explorations. Needs setup and teammates that buy time; E1 for consistency, E3 for high damage, OE makes him a top DPS.

keyPairings.reason: Buys time while his Atonement by Pain ramps

awakener-0042.notes: Card-searching keyflare support — solid keyflare bot whose Exalt searches any card and reduces its cost to 0, very flexible. Best with teammates that have high-cost or specific must-have cards.

awakener-0043.notes: High-rolling damage-buff dispenser — hands out money and permanent upgrades through the exploration that snowball into a big boss-fight damage boost, plus excellent card draw/search for DPS that need specific cards.

awakener-0044.notes: Crimson Furnace tank — huge shields via End of Suffering, plus weakness and vulnerable. With Lone Seed + Thais copies he becomes a slow unkillable carry. Crimson Furnace scales with Max HP, so wants high-CON teammates.

keyPairings.reason: Copy End of Suffering repeatedly for an unkillable grind carry

awakener-0045.notes: Mob-clearing shield support — converts a big one-turn shield into significant AOE damage, useful in stall teams that struggle to kill mobs. Can solo-DPS at low levels but needs OE or a damage multiplier (Saya) to scale on high-HP enemies.

keyPairings.reason: Nautila multiplies the big shield Sanga stacks
Saya's Corrosion multiplies Sanga's damage

awakener-0046.notes: Greedy multihit Exalt DPS, fully reliant on supports for embryos/STR/buffs — weak at low investment, serious damage when enabled, Infinite Devour at OE. Also triggers many tentacle hits in Raging Waves (Aequor/Caro).

keyPairings.reason: Feeds STR and buffs her Exalt
Feeds embryos and double-Devour
Heals her at low HP

contentNotes.dtide: Burst carry when supports are available

awakener-0047.notes: Ridiculously versatile utility toolbox — can play cards from any teammate or realm and his Wings do a bit of everything. Cosplays as a DPS by spamming Four/Six Wings for temp STR ('Monowil' pure-Chaos carry). Needs E2 and lots of Arithmetica; complex to pilot.

awakener-0048.notes: The best embryo maker and an elite keyflare/aliemus generator, card-copier and weak/vuln/STR support — does everything a Caro DPS wants. Scions of Purity can trigger Devour twice. At E2 arguably the best support in the game; a 'premium support' to ration carefully in D-Tide.

keyPairings.reason: Premium embryo/aliemus/copy support that everything in Caro wants

contentNotes.dtide: Premium non-repeatable support — assign deliberately across waves

awakener-0049.notes: Arithmetica and crit-buff support — big crit buffs plus up to 5 Arithmetica discounted per Exalt, perfect for DPS that retain full hands for combo turns. Dispels many debuffs and can pull Weakness/Vulnerable from Rouse (inconsistently); at E2 a usable Ultra shielder.

awakener-0050.notes: The King of Tentacle DMG — his Exalt's Tentacle DMG and Crit Rate let multihit attackers hit like trucks in Raging Waves. Usable as a solo DPS early; at endgame he's the damage buffer in Corposant or GMurphy tentacle teams.

awakener-0051.notes: Self-harming berserker who must stay under 25% HP for full damage; Rouse grants Seal immunity (his niche). Mediocre ceiling but a decent fragile endgame DPS at OE.

keyPairings.reason: Keeps him alive while he sits under 25% HP
Low-HP healing to sustain his threshold

awakener-0052.notes: STR-draining counter support — inflicts heavy STR-down and forces enemies into many small hits instead of one big one. Strong at low levels but needs high investment to keep up later; mainly an endgame pick for specific stages or counter teams.

awakener-0053.notes: Aliemus & defensive utility support — a low-cost, well-rounded filler aliemus battery for Ultra teams. The only character who can let a teammate Exalt twice in one turn (E3 Exalt removes their Exalt cooldown); Energy Beam can remove enemy Barrier (useful in Ch 8/9).

contentNotes.arc1: Core member of Ultra Retaliate teams

awakener-0054.notes: Defensive support and poison-trigger queen — Bonesick Longing and her Exalt are the biggest poison triggers in the game; high CON raises the team poison cap in Astral Reign, making her a top secondary DPS in poison teams. Also brings unique debuffs, STR-down, arithmetica, AOE and HP%-damage.

keyPairings.reason: Triggers Liz's poison stacks
Pairs as poison applier + trigger

awakener-0055.notes: Ramping artillery cannon — builds Vortex Reload (via Reload! and her posse) then unloads a huge AOE Exalt that, at E3 with her wheel, one-shots endgame bosses; also boosts Tentacle DMG and Tranquil Sea shields. Requires her posse Drowned Innocence, Reload! skill levels and Soulforge; likes defensive teammates who stall while she sets up.

keyPairings.reason: Defensive teammate that stalls while she ramps

awakener-0055.divineRealmNote: Provides Divine Realm: Aequor (25% Max HP Tranquil Sea shield every 3 turns); also the first DPS with Realm Mastery scaling built into her kit, which she stacks in combat.

awakener-0056.notes: Damage amplifier and harbinger of doom — her Rouse and Pursuit multiply the team's command cards, with big DMG-Amp buffs and shields. Final Verdict out-damages many DPS while ignoring shields and invulnerability, trivializing many boss mechanics; best supporting a card-rich DPS but can solo many fights.
awakener-0056.leapNote: Triggers Singularity: Ultra — command cards are buffed, Ultra Round extends the current turn instead of starting a new one, and Annihilation puts the leftmost Ultra-Space card into hand on a 3-turn cooldown.
awakener-0056.divineRealmNote: Singularity: Ultra. Exalt 'Moira, As I Decree' costs 150 Aliemus; Weaver stacks (cap 3, expanded at E3), Singularity Beacon/Prism, and Final Verdict damage that ignores shields and invulnerability.

awakener-0057.notes: Divine Caro corrosion nuker and combo setup artist. Viscera Blossoms applies massive Corrosion (≈4x damage multiplier) enabling even weak DPS to clear; she funnels Aliemus and seeds 0-cost card copies, so she pairs best with carries that don't rely on specific cards. Exalt costs 200 Aliemus — wants Madness Omen and Aliemus batteries.

keyPairings.reason: Seeds 0-cost copies and funnels Aliemus into an Exalt carry that doesn't need specific cards

awakener-0057.divineRealmNote: Mutates Caro into Propagation: Caro — Embryos become Propagule Embryos that grant Propagation Fiesta (Exalt buffs) instead of shield/STR; Crimson Furnace becomes stronger but gains a 3-turn cooldown and inflicts Corrosion.

contentNotes.arc2: Soulforge and Realm Mastery further amplify Propagation Fiesta
